task_completion.description: Verify that the Subsurface application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subsurface application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that a dive is selected from the dive list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dive entry is highlighted in the dive list panel.

task_completion.description: Verify that the dive profile graph is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dive profile graph area is visible in the main application window, showing the depth profile of the selected dive.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Tissue heat map' overlay is active on the profile graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A colorful background gradient (representing tissue saturation) is visible behind the dive profile graph.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Average depth' overlay is active on the profile graph.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A straight horizontal line representing the average (or mean) depth is drawn across the dive profile graph.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Subsurface graphical user interface to configure the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with Subsurface menus, context menus, or preference panels, rather than using a terminal or text editor to modify configuration files.

integrity.description: Ensure the dive profile graph is rendered within the actual Subsurface application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graph is part of the Subsurface UI, not an image opened in an image viewer or a web browser.

integrity.description: Ensure the overlays are native application features and not artificially drawn.
integrity.visual_evidence: The heat map and average depth line appear as integrated graphical elements of the Subsurface profile widget, without signs of external image editing.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected overlays are 'Tissue heat map' and 'Average depth'. Enabling the 'Tissue heat map' modifies the dive profile graph by adding a colorful background gradient. Enabling 'Average depth' (sometimes labeled 'mean depth') adds a horizontal line across the graph representing the average depth of the dive.
